scope.summary: Caps compile errors, describe constraints, and PL/Scope arrays, and makes the fixed DDL prefix explicitly loss-aware.
out_of_scope: The separate full tool-surface R2 byte-budget sweep.
Complete DDL continuation beyond the fixed 4000-character DBMS_METADATA prefix.

entry_points.name: oracle_get_ddl
entry_points.trace: get_ddl reads DBMS_LOB.GETLENGTH beside the fixed prefix and the dispatcher returns full and returned character counts, truncated, and a partial-DDL next step.

entry_points.name: oracle_compile_errors and oracle_describe
entry_points.trace: Both parse max_rows under a server ceiling, fetch one sentinel row, and return explicit truncation fields rather than inferring loss silently.

entry_points.name: oracle_plscope_inspect
entry_points.trace: Identifier and statement arrays are independently capped; a partial identifier graph suppresses the otherwise unsafe unused-declaration verdict.

limitations: A clean detached worktree at source.sha passed cargo fmt --all -- --check.
The clean-worktree cargo check -p oraclemcp --lib --locked was refused because its committed Cargo.lock requires an update; no clean-tree build pass is claimed.
The required R2 serialized-byte sweep was not run here and remains the independent acceptance lane.
